title: After The War
content:
Last Post sounded
    Across the mead
    To where he loitered
    With absent heed.
    Five years before
    In the evening there
    Had flown that call
    To him and his Dear.
    "You'll never come back;
    Good-bye!" she had said;
    "Here I'll be living,
    And my Love dead!"

    Those closing minims
    Had been as shafts darting
    Through him and her pressed
    In that last parting;
    They thrilled him not now,
    In the selfsame place
    With the selfsame sun
    On his war-seamed face.
    "Lurks a god's laughter
    In this?" he said,
    "That I am the living
    And she the dead!"
meaning:
"After The War" is a quintessential example of Thomas Hardy's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
